summary: DHH describes his evolving relationship with AI coding agents, explaining that autonomous terminal-based agents finally won him over after he was cold on the earlier autocomplete-style AI coding pioneered by Copilot and Cursor. He compares working with agents to managing a team rather than pair programming with an overzealous partner. While he's ready to promote AI agents to producing production-grade code, he pushes back on claims of 90%+ AI-written code and pure vibe coding, positioning 'supervised collaboration' as the realistic current paradigm. He urges programmers not to tune out the AI revolution despite legitimate skepticism about hype.
key_points: Autonomous terminal-based AI agents represent a fundamentally different and superior experience compared to in-editor autocomplete tools like Copilot and Cursor
The key shift is from AI as pair programmer stealing your keyboard to AI as a team member doing autonomous work you review
Supervised collaboration is production-ready today, but pure vibe coding remains aspirational for professional work
Claims of 90%+ AI-written code are wildly overstated if you hold the line on quality and cohesion
The improvement trajectory from 2025 is the most exciting computing development since the internet in the '90s
money_quotes: Reality is fast catching the hype!
When I code, I want to finish my own thoughts and sentences.
It's more like working on a team and less like working with an overly-zealous pair programmer who can't stop stealing the keyboard to complete the code you were in the middle of writing.
They're fully capable of producing production-grade contributions to real-life code bases.
Yet pure vibe coding remains an aspirational dream for professional work for me, for now. Supervised collaboration, though, is here today.

I'm nowhere close to the claims of having agents write 90%+ of the code, as I see some boast about online. I don't know what code they're writing to hit those rates, but that's way off what I'm able to achieve, if I hold the line on quality and cohesion.
This is the most exciting thing we've made computers do since we connected them to the internet back in the '90s.
Download OpenCode, throw some real work at Opus or the others, and relish the privilege of being alive during the days we taught the machines how to think.
key_insight: AI coding agents have earned a promotion from learning aids to production-grade team members, but supervised collaboration—not pure vibe coding—is the realistic paradigm for professional work today.
